CORRECTED SEPERATE STATEMENT OF FACTS IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MORAS, ARIANNA A MINOR BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIANS AD LITEM, JOHN MORAS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON GARRISON'S DECLARATION AND THE TESTIMONY REGARDING WHO WAS ON DUTY AS DESCRIBED IN THE DEPOSITIONS OF RAMARIZ, BUDNICK, HARRIS AND JONES. THERE ALSO ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING PRIOR INCIDENTS INVOLVING THE VICTIM. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON FAILING TO TAKE PRECAUTIONS TO PROTECT PLAINTIFF AND FOR THAT REASON THE MOTION IS DENIED. OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE SET FORTH ON THE RECORD AND NEED NOT BE INCLUDED IN THE ORDER AFTER HEARING. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S SUR-REPLY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT TABORA'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SUPPLEMENT DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. COUNSEL ARE TO SUBMIT AN AGREED UPON ORDER TO THE COURT WITHIN FIVE DAYS. IF THEY CANNOT AGREE UPON AN ORDER, INDIVIDUAL ORDERS ARE TO BE SUBMITTED. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: DIANA PADILLA, CSR 9975
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO DEFENDANT TABORA, WILLIAM TABORA, GODIN

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL PAPUC, ESQUIRE (415.773.1755) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ANITA L. RIMES, ESQUIRE (SHUPE & FINKELSTEIN, 650.341.3693) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT. TIM DOLAN, ESQUIRE (415.308.3396) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS TABORA.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON GARRISON'S DECLARATION AND THE TESTIMONY REGARDING WHO WAS ON DUTY AS DESCRIBED IN THE DEPOSITIONS OF RAMARIZ, BUDNICK, HARRIS AND JONES. THERE ALSO ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING PRIOR INCIDENTS INVOLVING THE VICTIM. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON FAILING TO TAKE PRECAUTIONS TO PROTECT PLAINTIFF AND FOR THAT REASON THE MOTION IS DENIED. OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE SET FORTH ON THE RECORD AND NEED NOT BE INCLUDED IN THE ORDER AFTER HEARING. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S SUR-REPLY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT TABORA'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SUPPLEMENT DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. COUNSEL ARE TO SUBMIT AN AGREED UPON ORDER TO THE COURT WITHIN FIVE DAYS. IF THEY CANNOT AGREE UPON AN ORDER, INDIVIDUAL ORDERS ARE TO BE SUBMITTED. (302/PJM/AP) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: DIANA PADILLA, CSR 9975

Notice Of Motion To Quash And For Protectice Order Regarding The Deposition Of Lisa Ochs And Demand For Inspection And Copying Of Documents And Things
Off Calendar Feb-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's motion to quash and for a protective order regarding the deposition of Lisa Ochs.
The motion to quash is granted. If Plaintiff re-issues the subpoena, the subpoena shall comply with CCP Section 1985.6 et seq. To assist the parties, the Court indicates that it would find good cause to permit a second deposition of the witness based upon the December 28, 2006 declaration. Counsel for plaintiff should be afforded the opportunity to question the witness as to the content of the declaration. Defendant's previous attempt to informally resolve the issue by offering to stipulate to a further deposition limited to the factual assertions or the factual basis in the declaration is unduly restrictive. Counsel for plaintiff is entitled to inquire as to any matter set forth in the declaration that is not privileged.

Based upon the moving papers submitted to date, the Court would find that an attorney-client relationship exists between counsel for defendant and the witness. Furthermore, the evidence does not demonstrate a waiver of the privilege based upon the statements of counsel, the crime-fraud exception, or a claim that the witness has been designated as a witness pursuant to CCP Section 2034. As to the items requested in the deposition subpoena, the Court is unable to rule upon any claim of privilege at this time because it is unclear what documents, if any, will be withheld. Similarly, until the witness testifies, the Court is unable to determine whether particular questions implicate the attorney client or attorney work product privilege. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Ntc Of Motion
Off Calendar Feb-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff' Ariana Moras' motion to compel the deposition of Lisa Ochs. The motion is moot in light of the ruling as to defendant's motion to quash and for a protective order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
